name: Heirloom Chocolate Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Butter the bottoms of three 9-inch pans.
3. Line the bottoms with waxed paper.
4. In a small bowl, mix flour, salt, and baking soda.
5. In a large bowl, cream butter until light.
6. Add sugar gradually and beat until fluffy.
7. Add eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition.
8. Beat in egg yolks and then melted chocolate.
9. Mix in dry ingredients alternately, beginning and ending with dry ingredients.
10. Divide batter between pans.
11. Bake until a wooden pick comes clean (about 25 minutes).
12. Place cakes on racks to cool for 5 minutes.
13. Unmold cakes using a pointed knife to loosen sides; place on paper towel-lined racks and cool completely.
14. Peel parchment off cooled cakes.
15. Place one cake layer on a platter, adding strips of waxed paper under the edges.
16. Spread frosting on top.
17. Repeat for other layers.
18. Add walnuts decoratively to the top edge and center.
